sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section VI, List of Answers on Topics 48:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section VI, List of Answers on Topics 48:1
items.html: The Parsa is considered the Sium Raglayim of the inner Partzuf. Like the Parsa inside the intestines of a person, which separates the breathing, vitality organs, from the feeding organs, so the Parsa separates the SAG above the Tabur from the other half of the Partzuf below the Tabur. This is so although these two halves are one Partzuf, but the Etzbaot Raglayim [toes] are phases of Sium on a whole Partzuf. (Inner Light, Item 9)
